transcript: --microwave oven, a 46 inch Sony big screen television set, and finally, a two thousand dollar cashier's check made out to you.
Now, Mike, knowing that one of those five awards is definitely yours, I'd like to say congratulations again. How does all that sound for a lot of great news today?

Alright, fantastic. Now, like any other promotion, there are only two things we would like to see you do for us while you're in the process of claiming your award. One is, after you receive the award, take a picture of yourself with it, send that back to us, so we can use it for promotional purposes.
Now, in approximately sixty days, the company will be calling you back with shipping instructions on one of those five fabulous awards.

Now, second, like any other promotion, we ask that you become a first time customer of ours by trying one of our health and energy products. What we're using is a tested and proven vitamin and mineral program called Universal Life. Now, Mike, do you currently take vitamins?

Okay, fantastic. Mike, these are the finest vitamins on the American market today. Of course, that is my opinion. Uh, I do take them, as well as my wife and children. Okay, that's five tablets a day, they supply most of your daily requirements from A to zinc. Now, these are non-allergenic and they are derived from all natural products.

It also has what's called a special sustained time release formula, now, that ensures the maximum benefit throughout the day. Now, we have these boxed up in a twelve month supply. During the promotion, Mike, you're receiving that vitamin program for exactly six hundred and ninety-eight dollars. In the same package is going to be your major award certificate, that officially guarantees one of the five major awards. Now Mike, have you ever won a major award?
Fabulous! Well, Mike, your good fortune is continuing, and I'll tell you what, if you get that Cadillac Coupe Deville, you better send me a bottle of champagne!
Okay, now, it won't be a problem getting a picture of you or a loved one with the award, correct?
Uh, there is something standing in my way.
Okay... uh... what would be the best time of day for us to deliver?
Umm... uh, could you talk to the Parochial Nimrod?
What are you talking about, Mike?
Well, that's the thing that's standing in my way. I--
I'm... I'm... I'm afraid I don't know how to handle it.
You don't know how to handle what?
Mike, I... what... what are you talkin' about?
What... what... how to handle this from here on.
You don't know how to handle what? The award?
Okay. Mike, what I need is the expiration date on the Visa.
What is the expiration date on your Visa?
Okay, now, what I need... there's some... what's going on over there? I keep hearing... seems like you're fading out or something.
Okay. What I need is the long number that begins with a four. Read that to me slowly--
I need the long number that begins with a four, Mike.
Yeah. Okay, what I need is the long number that begins with a four.
[exasperated] Mike! What is going on over there?!
The Parochial Ramrod? What are you... what are you doin'?
Well, I'm trying to tell you, sir, that that's going to pose a problem.
That's the only problem, is your Parochial Rimrod?
Uhh... am I st... am I still eligible to be a recipient of these fine awards?
Yes, what I need is the long number that begins with a four.
Long number that begins with a four.
[laughing] Long number that begins with a four.
What I need is the long number that begins with a four, Mike.
Mike... Mike, where is your Parochial Rimrod? Where is that?
Um... I've... I think I might've told you the wrong thing. It's, um... I think it's a Rhinoceros Dickrod.
Mike, did you take your medication today?
Mike, what I need, go... go grab your Visa...
...read me the long number that begins with a four.
Okay. Grab your Visa. Do you understand what's going on?
Okay, read me the long number that begins with a four.

Mike, my name is Frank Mathis, I'm the promotional director. If you are not interested in taking part in this promotion, simply tell us you're not interested. This is a long distance call, that we are paying for.

Either you're interested, or you're not interested. If ya are interested, what we are gonna need to finish up the paperwork would be the account number beginning with a four on the Visa. D'ya have that information handy?
Okay, well, what we're gonna need is the long number that begins with a four.
Alright, do you have that there?
Okay, Mike, ya have some sort of noise interference. I can't tell you what it is--
Okay. Now, you got the number begins with a four?
Alright. Read that to me slowly.
Alright, now... now read the number that begins with a four.
I can accommodate you there, sir.
Okay, well, Mike, again, if you're not interested, simply say you're not interested. [indiscernible]
I'd have to be crazy to turn down these wonderful prizes.

Well, again, Mike, again, it... uh... we do not appreciate the ridicule. If you're not interested, just forget the sarcasm. All you got to do is simply say "I'm not interested." If ya are interested, then this is what we're gonna need. [indiscernible]
Well, uh... do I have to provide a gridicular buttrod?
If the bill was going on your number, then again, I think you would be a little more serious with me.
Well, I need a gridicular giraffe rod.
Mike, do you have any idea what's going on here? Do you have any idea what... what we're explaining to ya?
Um.... you mentioned something about a Gary Coleman nimrod?
Mike, Mike, are you under any kind of medication?
Have you taken any medication today?
If you're under some form of... of medication, I can't get back in touch with ya.
I can't. Okay, I'm not gonna, you know... I don't want ya going into a seizure or anything if you need to take any medication right now.
What I can do is I can give ya a call back, some time when you're a little more sedated.
Okay, well, again, then uh... then, again, we go over this round and around. All I'm gonna need, if you are interested, is the number that begins with the four.

Now, read me the number. This is the last time I'm going to ask ya, Mike. If you're not interested, tell me you're not interested. If you're interested right now, read me the number beginning with the four.
Okay... um... it's in... It's in my saxophone, I'm gonna have to dig it out.
Mike, again, I mean, you can joke all you like. Alright? Okay?
You can joke all you wanna joke, again, that's completely up to you. It's not my award. If you don't want it, we will simply give it to someone else in your area.
Well, my... it's... the card's in my saxophone.
Uh huh. Well, Mike, do you have any clue how ridiculous ya sound?
Well, I don't think that's very nice.
Well, again, Mike. I mean, either, you... you... two seconds ago ya have the card there, now ya don't have the card.
Oh, alright, Mike. Well, listen, I'm gonna be honest with ya.
You know, if you had any clue to what you're talking about, or any idea what we're doing here for ya here today, I know you would've participated.
Well, I'm trying to be a participant.
Well no, no, Mike, you're not trying to. Alright, if ya are interested, then simply read me the number beginning with the four. This is the way we bill the account for the amount of the charge.
I dropped it in my xylophone.
Oh, alright. Well, Mike, what I can suggest you do is get yourself some medical help. Okay?
Okay, well again, I don't appreciate--
--the sarcasm. If you're not interested simply tell us--
I don't think that's very friendly.

Mike, you're... I'm telling ya Mike, you're hysterical. And you know, I'd love to sit here and waste my valuable time on ya, but unfortunately I have a board meeting to go to. I do want you to know that ya are turning the award down.
Well, Mike, again, unless you're going to cooperate with me here, and give me the information I need in order to fill out the paperwork, I am going to have to let you go.
How about if I perform, uh... taekwondo on you?
Well, again, Mike, if that's what you feel you'd like to do, feel free, okay? If you could perform your taekwondo over the phone, again, I'd really love to see that.
Yo, shut the fuck up, Chico, man!
